title: Goughia laurina
taxonomy: Goughia laurina is classified within the plant family Proteaceae. This family consists of flowering plants native to Australia, New Zealand, and Southern Africa. Goughia laurina, specifically, is an endangered shrub native to New South Wales in Australia. It belongs to the genus Goughia, which is a small group of flowering plants. The classification of Goughia laurina is further categorized within the order Proteales.

intro: Goughia laurina, also known as the Goughia tree, is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, native to the subtropical regions of Australia. The health benefits of Goughia laurina include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
